title: Implement rule-based technical analysis system with 6-step objective process

requirements.summary: Document 6-step rule-based technical analysis process requirements in API configuration
acceptanceCriteria: apiConfig.ts includes detailed comments documenting the 6-step technical analysis process requirements
Comments specify objective rule-based approach with no subjective interpretation
Documentation includes scoring system rules and signal thresholds
Comments note that unclear cases should return 'SEM ENTRADA' instead of fabricated signals

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive JSDoc comments above API endpoint configurations documenting the mandatory 6-step technical analysis process: 1) image preprocessing (contrast enhancement, high sharpness conversion, candle detection, wick/body/high/low identification, broker UI element filtering), 2) candle reading (extract direction, body size, wick size, color sequence, movement strength), 3) trend identification (ascending tops/bottoms = ALTA, descending = BAIXA, sideways = LATERAL), 4) price action pattern detection with rule confirmation (engulfing, hammer, shooting star, doji, pullback, breakout, support/resistance), 5) signal scoring system (+2 strong trend, +1 favorable pattern, -1 resistance, -2 contrary pattern; >= 2 = COMPRA, <= -2 = VENDA, -1 to 1 = NEUTRO), 6) mandatory return structure (trend, patterns, score, confidence %, signal, technical explanation). Include explicit note that unclear cases must return 'SEM ENTRADA' rather than fabricated signals. Emphasize objective rule-based approach without subjective interpretation.

requirements.summary: Extend API type definitions to support score field and additional signal values
acceptanceCriteria: ApiAnalysisResponse interface includes optional 'pontuacao?: number' field
The 'sinal' field type accepts 'COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'NEUTRO' | 'SEM ENTRADA'
JSDoc comments document the scoring system and signal thresholds
Type definitions align with the 6-step analysis requirements

file_operations.description: Add optional 'pontuacao?: number' field to ApiAnalysisResponse interface to store the total score from the 6-step analysis. Update the 'sinal' field type from its current definition to accept 'COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'NEUTRO' | 'SEM ENTRADA' as valid string literal values. Add comprehensive JSDoc comments above the interface explaining the scoring system (+2/-2 point scale), signal thresholds (>= 2 for COMPRA, <= -2 for VENDA, -1 to 1 for NEUTRO), and that 'SEM ENTRADA' indicates no clear trading signal was detected by the rule-based analysis.

requirements.summary: Update API response mapper to handle NEUTRO and SEM ENTRADA signals
acceptanceCriteria: mapApiResponse function handles 'NEUTRO' signal mapping to internal format
mapApiResponse function handles 'SEM ENTRADA' signal mapping to internal format
Score field (pontuacao) is preserved if present in API response
Mapping logic does not break existing COMPRA/VENDA handling

file_operations.description: Extend the signal mapping logic to handle 'NEUTRO' by mapping it to AnalysisDirection.sideways in the internal AnalysisResult format. Add handling for 'SEM ENTRADA' signal value, mapping it to a recognizable internal state (consider using sideways with a flag or special confidence value). Preserve the 'pontuacao' field from ApiAnalysisResponse if present, storing it in an appropriate location in the AnalysisResult structure (may need to add field or store in metadata). Ensure existing COMPRA/VENDA signal mappings continue to work correctly. Update any signal validation or switch statements to include all four signal types.

requirements.summary: Update SignalCard component to display NEUTRO, SEM ENTRADA signals and score
acceptanceCriteria: SignalCard renders NEUTRO signals with neutral styling
SignalCard renders SEM ENTRADA signals with warning styling and Portuguese text
Total score (pontuacao) is displayed when available with 'Pontuação:' label
All four signal types have distinct visual appearances

file_operations.description: Add visual styling and rendering logic for NEUTRO signals using neutral colors (gray or yellow indicator) with appropriate Portuguese label ('Neutro' or 'Mercado Neutro'). Add visual styling for SEM ENTRADA signals using warning/info style with Portuguese text 'Sem sinal de entrada detectado'. Add conditional rendering to display the total score (pontuacao) when available in the analysis result, with label 'Pontuação:' followed by the numeric value. Ensure all four signal types (COMPRA, VENDA, NEUTRO, SEM ENTRADA) have distinct visual indicators using different colors, icons, or styling. Update any existing signal type conditionals or switch statements to handle all cases.

requirements.summary: Update Results page to handle NEUTRO and SEM ENTRADA signals with appropriate messaging
acceptanceCriteria: Results page displays appropriate Portuguese messaging for NEUTRO signals
Results page displays appropriate Portuguese messaging for SEM ENTRADA signals
SEM ENTRADA results have modified UI indicating no actionable signal
Score (pontuacao) is displayed prominently when available

file_operations.description: Add conditional rendering logic to detect NEUTRO signal type and display appropriate Portuguese messaging ('Mercado em consolidação - aguarde confirmação') in a prominent location on the results page. Add conditional rendering for SEM ENTRADA signal type displaying clear Portuguese messaging ('Nenhum sinal claro detectado - aguarde melhor oportunidade') and consider hiding or disabling the 'Save Analysis' button for SEM ENTRADA results to indicate no actionable signal. Add prominent display of the score (pontuacao) field when present in the analysis result, positioned near the main signal indicator. Update any existing signal-specific UI sections to handle all four signal types appropriately.

requirements.summary: Update analysis API service to communicate objective analysis requirements
acceptanceCriteria: analysisApi.ts includes configuration or comments documenting objective analysis requirement
Request payload or headers can convey analysis methodology requirements to API
Code includes notes that API should return 'SEM ENTRADA' for unclear cases
Implementation does not break existing API call functionality

file_operations.description: Add configuration comments or JSDoc above the API call function documenting that the external API must follow objective rule-based analysis without subjective interpretation and must implement the 6-step technical process. Consider adding an instruction field to the request payload or custom header (e.g., 'X-Analysis-Mode: objective-rules') to communicate analysis methodology requirements to the API provider. Include code comments noting that the API must return 'SEM ENTRADA' for unclear cases rather than fabricating signals. Ensure changes do not break existing API call functionality, retry logic, or timeout handling.

requirements.summary: Update History components to display NEUTRO and SEM ENTRADA signals
acceptanceCriteria: History list displays NEUTRO signals with 'Neutro' label and neutral styling
History list displays SEM ENTRADA signals with 'Sem entrada' label and distinct styling
Historical entries for all signal types are clearly distinguishable
List item component handles all four signal types without errors

file_operations.description: Review the history list rendering logic to ensure it properly passes all signal types including NEUTRO and SEM ENTRADA to the HistoryListItem component. Add any necessary filtering or display logic specific to handling these new signal types in the history view. Ensure the component properly renders items with all four signal types without errors.

Add rendering logic and visual styling to display NEUTRO signals with Portuguese label 'Neutro' and neutral styling (gray or similar neutral color scheme) to differentiate from buy/sell signals. Add rendering logic for SEM ENTRADA signals with Portuguese label 'Sem entrada' and distinct styling (e.g., warning/info color scheme) to indicate no actionable signal was generated. Update any signal type conditionals, switch statements, or icon selections to handle all four signal types (COMPRA, VENDA, NEUTRO, SEM ENTRADA). Ensure each signal type is clearly distinguishable in the history list view through color, icon, or styling differences.
